examples: Cultist doctrine line: "We build the lanes" -> "We build the routes" to keep the reveal in living speech rather than planner diction.
Antonidas contact: "We have the architect" -> "We have the name" so Jaina sounds pressured and precise, not like she is filing a report.
observations: Opening paragraphs 1-13 already matched the style target: dry, tactical, dialogue-forward, and close to Jaina's problem-focused attention.
Sylvanas's readiness beat in paragraphs 8-10 stays in Jaina's lane by showing what Jaina reads in Sylvanas's body instead of explaining Sylvanas from outside.
Interrogation paragraphs 61-92 kept Jaina in full mage register without drifting into modern lab language; "preserving shell" stayed earned.
The prisoner doctrine reveal in paragraphs 125-127 had one voice break: "lanes" sounded like plan scaffolding, not something a covert operative would say under pressure.
The Antonidas exchange in paragraphs 351-371 needed the most voice work; the revised phrasing now sounds like Jaina speaking to her mentor in crisis rather than summarizing evidence into bullets.

examples: Map revelation: "No one interrupted." -> "Even Corin kept still." The silence is now rendered through something Jaina can directly observe.
observations: Opening trap sequence paragraphs 3-27 stays close-limited: Jaina tracks ward-thread, metal anchors, breath timing, and magical suppression rather than neutral scene coverage.
Interrogation paragraphs 83-91 keep the prisoner's fear inside Jaina's available evidence: sweat, pulse, and the upward direction of his panic.
Remote-kill paragraphs 139-154 remain in Jaina's epistemology; she feels the strike as a narrow necromantic line and reaches for life too late, without any forbidden view of the caster.
The cellar realization passage originally used one tiny neutral-narrator sentence; grounding the silence in Corin's stillness keeps the moment fully inside Jaina's noticed world.
Antonidas's reaction is handled cleanly through pause, cloth movement, and stripped speech, so the prose never claims more certainty about his inner state than Jaina can infer.

notes: Chapter 7's same-night capture handoff holds cleanly; chapter 8 opens on the promised west-line snatch without recap drag.
Corin and Havel remain local Andorhal allies, not early members of the later traveling field cast.
Kel'Thuzad is not named until after the rollout realization, preserving the plan constraint.
Lyriel remains alive through the chapter close and stays functionally distinct from Cyndia in the escape sequence.
Antonidas is informed before chapter close, locking the Kirin Tor thread the verifier required for chapter 8.
observations: Prior chapter residue is visible from the first page: same night, same city, same capture plan, same trusted split labor.
The grain-web knowledge matches the memory files: Andorhal is confirmed as an active hub, not falsely upgraded into the whole command structure.
The chapter correctly preserves Jaina's late-book frustration pattern: knowledge arrives, but too late to save the piece directly in front of her.
The Kel'Thuzad reveal arrives from fragments shared across Corin, Havel, the dead operative, and Jaina's institutional memory, matching the plan's provenance rules.
The chapter close properly leaves Corin and Havel behind in the city's continuity lane while the core four-woman unit points north and east.

examples: Map-turn section: broke the discovery into "Jaina looked at the map... / Plain enough to hurt." so the realization lands with a sharper cadence shift.
observations: The opening action run uses the style-guide's preferred short-medium alternation well: brisk setup, one-line beats, then a quick return to motion.
Interrogation paragraphs 47-127 already varied sentence length effectively, especially where Sylvanas's clipped questions cut across Jaina's longer interpretive lines.
The escape passage paragraphs 173-231 maintains pressure without flattening into pure staccato; longer route sentences are used only when geography needs clarity.
The rollout realization around paragraphs 279-289 was the weakest rhythm patch; it wanted one harder break before Jaina's conclusion fully arrived.
The Antonidas call and final three paragraphs preserve the chapter's silence-heavy cadence, letting short lines do the emotional work instead of explanatory prose.

examples: Antonidas report beat: "We have the architect" -> "We have the name" so the handoff preserves the chapter's fragment-built certainty instead of overstating solved command knowledge.
continuity.crossChapter: Verified against chapter7/final.md: same-night carry is clean and chapter 8 acknowledges chapter 7's closing operation without cooling beat.
observations: Capture to interrogation is fully causal: they take the man alive, so the next immediate pressure is what fear can be made to yield.
Interrogation to remote kill lands cleanly on the prisoner's almost-confession; the city answers before he can finish the sentence.
Remote kill to street escape keeps the emotional residue of shock alive; the room flips from questioning site to compromised marker with no false pause.
Shelter analysis to rollout revelation follows the plan's question-pressure: once escape buys breath, the chapter asks the answer Jaina most wants and least wants confirmed.
The Antonidas stone beat now honors the chapter's logic: they have enough to move north, but not enough to pretend the case is cleaner than it is.

examples: Abstract reveal texture trimmed: the map-turn now lands in concrete physical language instead of "stripped at last of any flattering lie."
Neutral summary silence trimmed: replaced a generic quiet beat with Corin's visible stillness.
observations: Scene openings were already strong: no weather throat-clearing, no waking-up filler, no generic room-entry scaffolding.
The chapter had very little leftover slophunter damage; most surviving issues were small abstractions rather than heavy AI-pattern clusters.
The weakest prose patch sat in the cellar revelation, where one abstract sentence and one neutral silence sentence softened the blow of the discovery.
The chapter's better lines were left intact on purpose: "Knowledge, again. Clean, hard, late." and the final hardness line both earn their compression.
No continuity-breaking over-exposition or callback re-introduction survived into the closing pages; the prose trusts the reader's memory of Andorhal's role.

examples: Prisoner doctrine: "We build the lanes" -> "We build the routes."
Jaina on the response net: "they already had lanes in place" -> "they already had their people in place."
Jaina to Antonidas: "We have the architect" -> "We have the name."
Jaina's proof recital to Antonidas was rewritten from briefing cadence into pressured spoken reasoning built from Corin, Havel, the dead operative, and the structure of the work.
observations: Most dialogue already passed the plain-language ceiling, especially Sylvanas, Havel, and Corin; they sound like people in a room, not memo writers.
Jaina's full mage register remained mostly clean because the technical terms are arcane and embodied rather than clinical or bureaucratic.
The two uses of "lanes" were the clearest surviving contamination because they sounded imported from the plan schema rather than from lived speech.
The Antonidas exchange needed the strongest register correction because mentor-stone scenes punish any hint of summary-report language.
I kept "preserving shell" and "field necromancers" because both remain setting-native, character-earned, and orally plausible in this exact scene.

researchChecks: Andorhal remains aligned with canon as Lordaeron's grain-distribution hub and breadbasket node.
Kel'Thuzad's Kirin Tor expulsion and Cult role remain aligned with canon sources and the project's own character file.
Cult structure and plague-cell behavior remain plausible for covert Lordaeron infiltration.
Antonidas/Jaina mentor dynamics still fit pre-Third-War canon expectations.
Eastweald, Caer Darrow, and the north-of-Andorhal escalation point remain geographically coherent.
metaAudit: Re-read the interrogation, the cellar revelation, and the Antonidas stone call as the chapter's three weakest sections.
Interrogation needed only one diction correction; pressure and power shifts were already strong.
The cellar revelation wanted a harder physical landing, so I sharpened the cadence and removed one abstract sentence.
The Antonidas exchange needed the most work because the scene's institutional weight made any briefing-language slip immediately audible.
No deeper rewrite was warranted; the chapter is already craft-level prose and mainly needed line-level sharpening.
